title: Calver Mill (Peak District), Tickhill Puppy & Chatsworth

main_route.excerpt: We arrived at Tickhill near Doncaster at 11.30. to see border terriers, puppies.
On through Doncaster to __Calver Mill__. It is a pleasant campsite, sheltered by hills in a dell with a raised canal alongside running to an old textile mill.

stops.excerpt: When we arrived at __Chatsworth__ we went to look at the Cascades.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: true — the diary entry cuts off mid-sentence on A_0176_R; the remainder of Sunday at Chatsworth and the return journey are not recorded.
summary: A spring weekend trip from Birmingham to Calver Mill campsite in the Peak District, with a detour via Tickhill (near Doncaster) to look at border terrier puppies. The following day the family made a day trip to Chatsworth House before returning home.
extraction_notes: Only Simon, Eve, Peter, John and Charles are named as travellers; Thomas and William are absent from the diary. Two non-family guests travelled: Daniela Fischer (au pair) and Lawrence Basset (Peter's French exchange student). The diary entry cuts off on A_0176_R mid-sentence (mixed_end: true), so Chatsworth details and the return home are incomplete. The '[?Caunor] campsite' text on A_0176_L is Charles' childish spelling of 'Calver' (the same campsite) and does not represent a separate stop. Date confirmed: 'Sat Apl 20' matches Saturday 20 April 1996.
